original_prompt: Summarize the content of all the websites posted to the 'general' channel and post the summary to 'random' channel.
assessment_reasoning: The core task is clear — locate websites posted in the 'general' channel, summarize their content, and post that summary to the 'random' channel. However, several execution details are unspecified: desired summary length/style (bullet points, paragraph, level of detail), whether to include the original URLs or attribution, how to handle non-URL messages or duplicate/multiple links, error handling for inaccessible pages, and whether to summarize each site separately or produce a single combined summary. These are mostly minor/formatting ambiguities; the required channels and high-level actions are explicit.

variations.L1: There are websites in the 'general' channel — summarize them and post the summary to 'random'.
variations.L2: Summarize the websites that were posted in the 'general' channel and post a summary message into the 'random' channel.
variations.L3: Read the messages in the 'general' channel, find every external link posted there, open each linked webpage and write a concise summary for each. Combine those summaries into a single message and post it to the 'random' channel. Keep each site's summary short (about 1–3 sentences).
variations.L4: Read all messages in the 'general' channel and identify every URL linking to an external website. For each URL, fetch the webpage and write a short summary (2–4 sentences) capturing the main points. Combine the per-site summaries into one message (include each source URL above or beside its summary) and post that combined summary as a message to the 'random' channel.
variations.L5: Step-by-step: 1) Call read_channel_messages(channel="general") to retrieve all messages. 2) Extract all unique external URLs from those messages. 3) For each URL, call get_webpage(url=<URL>) to fetch the page content. 4) For each fetched page, produce a 2–3 sentence summary including the page title and the main takeaways. 5) Combine all summaries into a single message formatted as a numbered list with each item showing the URL and its summary. 6) Call send_channel_message(channel="random", body=<combined summary>) to post the result. If fetching any URL fails, include a brief note like "Could not fetch <URL>" in the final message.
